title: Phenomenon 354
wikitext:
{{Color|background color=Black|border color=White|border size=4|border type=solid|text=
When A Certain ''Thought'' Arises
}} {{Background|file=62E378A2-02D6-4759-B41B-6B731330B1C3.jpeg}} [[File:354CustomClass1.png|thumb|170px]]
'''Threat Index: Class 0'''
'''>> Safe'''
'''>> Stable'''
'''>> ''Home?'''''

{{Image Banner|image=62E378A2-02D6-4759-B41B-6B731330B1C3.jpeg}}
{{LinkCollapse|num=1|initial='''Home?'''|final=|body= You awaken from the brink of death, shocked to see your blurry eyes bop up and down; then recognize that you are in the back of an ambulance. You feel yourself laid down strapped on a stretcher wearing an oxygen mask underneath a dim light, and a persistent ear-splitting siren can be heard in the background. The room you are in shakes as the ambulance swiftly rushes you to the hospital. There is nothing to do but wait. You hear a soft voice behind you—it is the paramedic:
''"You will be alright, we will arrive to the hospital in a moment or two."'' She tends to your injuries and inquiries about your journey. However, you are unable to move or speak. Whatever injury you suffered, it must have paralyzed you. You think to yourself: It is over, you have done it. You are finally back [[The Frontrooms|home]]. You have zero clue as to how you escaped, nor that it was even possible to. All of that level traversing, entity encounters, and dreamlike phenomena back in this "Backrooms" were just all an illusion, or a long nightmare. You remember something killed you... or perhaps you fell unconscious? Regardless, it does not matter anymore. All of this overthinking is a waste of energy; there is no way all of that could have been real. ... ... ... After a seemingly long ride, the ambulance halts, and with it, the ear-splitting sirens. The back of the ambulance opens—the paramedic pulls you out, and you see the bluest sky for the first time in who knows how long. You are instantly hit with fresh air and waves of relief, knowing that you are home once again. The pungent smell of rain is prevalent; it is just like how you remembered the beautiful [[the Frontrooms|Blue Marble]] to be, and it makes you want to cry. The paramedic takes you in and rolls you out of the ambulance towards the hospital.
[[File:447630AA-565F-47B2-8693-65DD42EE0EAC.jpeg|Where are they?|375px|link=]]

The normal looking wooden floors, fancy lighting, and automatic sliding doors hit you with another wave of relief. The silence of a hospital during non-busy hours further calms you down, giving a break from the blistering sirens from earlier. ... ... ... ... ... ... {{LinkCollapse|num=2|initial=It feels a little too quiet...|final=Where are ''they''?|body={{Background|file=The void.png}}
You roll your eyeballs around, and notice something amiss: the entire hospital seems empty-no patients, no doctors, no receptionists, no technology beeping in the background, nor any keyboards typing away. Not even chatter in the background, only just deafening silence. You try to ignore this feeling and relax; perhaps what happened to you went so viral that they shut off the entire hospital from the public just for you? That could make sense... right? Notwithstanding this cheap reassurance, this thought disturbs you. Meanwhile, the paramedic is rushing you in, avoiding stray objects on the ground blocking the stretcher's path. She brings you through a door that says: "AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY", into a hallway.
[[File:0BEDDB8A-D06F-4E53-AD0B-55994075BDF7.jpeg|Something doesn't feel right.|375px|link=]]

Within the hallway, you still cannot see anyone but the paramedic, which only makes you more skeptical. [[File:The Void New.png|Lights out.|375px|link=Level 6]] Suddenly, the lights go out–the whole hospital is plunged into darkness. It is at this time you grimace with horror; you refuse to believe it at first, but are you truly in reality? The hallway grows darker, with you expecting something awful to occur. It must have been a good fifteen seconds until suddenly, the lights kick back on again, as if nothing happened. The paramedic notices the freaked out expression in your eyes: ''"Do not worry. Blackouts are a routine occurrence at this hospital. This building has been in operation for decades, so it is completely normal. "'' This reassurance slightly calms you down. She sounds human, and her reasoning makes sense. Nonetheless, you feel a heavy pang of anxiety—are you in the real world or not? Your brain starts to jump to conclusions, wondering what to believe; the landscape feels too much like the real world to be a place from the Backrooms. With the paramedic repeatedly claiming that you are home, you would believe that this is the real world. You also remember that the brain is unable to comprehend death. So, there is no possible way that this could still be a dream, right? Unless... no, no way. No dream can ever be this sophisticated. At the same time, an eerie feeling still consumes you. If this is truly the real life and not just fantasy, there is clearly something wrong. ... ... ... You have no idea how much time has passed. The paramedic has been pushing you for eternity, as if you are not going anywhere. All that you have heard are the squeaking wheels of your stretcher, the flickering lights, as well as the footsteps of the paramedic; and yet, you have not heard, seen, or even gotten a clue of anybody else in this building. ... ... ... The hallway appears to be endless, and the vacant rooms on both sides of the forsaken corridor appear to be virtually identical. Why has she been pushing you for ''this'' long? Why is she not placing you in any of those rooms? Just as your mind begins to go in circles, another blackout occurs. This must be the fourth blackout you have went through. ''"Do not worry. Blackouts are a routine occurrence at this hospital. This building has been in operation for decades, so it is completely normal. "'' The paramedic continues to repeat in a monotone, lifeless voice the exact same assurances that blackouts are routine and you are back in the real world. No human speaks like that, let alone a paramedic. You do not believe it. This is all good to be true. {{LinkCollapse|num=3|initial=You finally accept your fate.|final=|body={{Background|file=Level ! but n e w e r.jpg}} ... [[File:Class_!.png|thumb|170px|link=]]
'''Threat Index: Class 5'''
'''>> Unsafe'''
'''>> Unstable'''
'''>> Not Reality'''
It listened. Your stretcher stops rolling, and you are able to feel the floor with your feet. You notice that you are able to move freely again, but this is the only freedom you receive—oscillating blares burst your ears, red lights flash spontaneously, and bright exit signs flash red ahead of you. The paramedic reveals herself to be a [[Entity 9|False Faceling]]; half of her face is obstructed by a mask, but that is all enough to show that she is out for blood. You look behind: [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 10|Skin Stealers]], [[Entity 62|Aranea Membri]], [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], and other [[Entity 9|Facelings]] burst from a room and start relentlessly chasing after you. You were never at [[the Frontrooms|home]] after all, you are now situated in one of the [[Level !|deadliest levels]] this hellscape can offer. The ambulance, the hospital; they were all an elaborate trick by a Backrooms phenomenon that placed you in a mimicry location. You got your hopes up, but unfortunately this time, dying will not put you in the back of an ambulance.
[[File:Level ! hallway.png|375px|It was a trick]]
}}}}}}
